div.head: The Ambassador in Japan (Grew) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
392. Our 381 September 17, 5 p.m. war bombing. We have just received the reply of the Foreign Office, the complete text of which will be telegraphed as soon as translated.Not printed. The reply expresses the “sincere regrets” of the Japanese Government for the occurrence
which was said to have been due to confusion of hospital with Chinese wireless station and it adds that the Japanese Government is prepared to give favorable consideration to indemnifying the hospital and its employees.A check in settlement of this claim (Chinese $7,957.00) was received by the Embassy from the Japanese Foreign Office in April 1938 (393.1163 Seventh Day Adventist/70).
Repeated to Shanghai for relay to Nanking.
